titleEn: Thailand and U.S. strengthen cooperation on produce pesticide testing
titleZh: 泰美加强果蔬农药残留检测合作，推动监管标准对接
summaryZh: 泰国食品药品监督管理局与美国农业部官员举行双边会谈，重点讨论进口新鲜果蔬和坚果的农药残留检测标准对接、边境检查系统协调、实验室分析方法以及超标处理程序。双方旨在通过技术交流和数据共享，提升消费者保护水平，同时支持国际贸易。该合作有助于建立更科学的监管框架，但对智慧畜牧领域（如IoT、AI、自动化）的直接关联较弱。
featuredReason: 虽然该新闻主要聚焦食品安全监管合作，而非直接涉及智慧畜牧技术，但农药残留检测的标准化和数据共享机制可为智慧农业中的质量追溯和自动化检测系统提供政策参考，值得关注跨境贸易合规的从业者阅读。
contentFull: Thailand and U.S. strengthen cooperation on produce pesticide testing The Thai Food and Drug Administration (FDA) has held bilateral talks with United States agricultural officials to strengthen safety surveillance of imported fresh fruit, vegetables, and nuts. The meeting brought together representatives from the Thai FDA, the United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) Agricultural Attaché, and the Assisting Specialty Crop Exports (ASCE) initiative. Discussions focused on aligning regulatory standards and strengthening consumer protection related to pesticide residues. Dr Rungruethai Muanprasitporn, deputy secretary-general of the Thai FDA, hosted the delegation at the agency's One-Stop Service Centre in Bangkok. The discussions focused on harmonising border inspection systems and improving understanding of each country's food safety framework. Key topics included sampling methods at ports of entry, laboratory analysis for chemical residues, and enforcement procedures when imported consignments exceed statutory maximum residue limits (MRLs). Dr Rungruethai said coordination between regulatory agencies is important to maintain confidence in the domestic food supply chain. The U.S. delegation outlined its regulatory approach for monitoring and managing chemical residues in agricultural exports. The Thai FDA said the technical exchange is expected to support long-term scientific cooperation, allowing Thai inspectors to access U.S. compliance data and data-sharing networks. "This meeting marks an excellent opportunity to strengthen technical cooperation and share regulatory experiences between Thailand and the United States," Dr Rungruethai concluded. He added that future policy updates would remain based on scientific principles to protect public health while supporting international trade. translationZh: 泰国食品药品监督管理局与美国农业部官员举行双边会谈，以加强对进口新鲜水果、蔬菜和坚果的安全监管。会议汇集了泰国FDA、美国农业部农业参赞以及特色作物出口援助倡议的代表。讨论聚焦于协调监管标准、加强消费者保护，特别是针对农药残留问题。泰国FDA副秘书长Rungruethai Muanprasitporn博士在曼谷该机构的一站式服务中心接待了代表团。双方重点讨论了边境检查系统的协调、增进对彼此食品安全框架的理解。关键议题包括入境口岸的抽样方法、化学残留的实验室分析，以及当进口货物超过法定最大残留限量时的执法程序。Rungruethai博士表示，监管机构之间的协调对于维持国内食品供应链的信心至关重要。美方代表团概述了其在农产品出口中监测和管理化学残留的监管方法。泰国FDA表示，此次技术交流有望支持长期的科学合作，使泰国检查员能够获取美国合规数据和数据共享网络。Rungruethai博士总结道：“这次会议是加强技术合作、分享泰美监管经验的绝佳机会。”他补充说，未来的政策更新将继续基于科学原则，以保护公众健康，同时支持国际贸易。
contentHtml: The Thai Food and Drug Administration (FDA) has held bilateral talks with United States agricultural officials to strengthen safety surveillance of imported fresh fruit, vegetables, and nuts. The meeting brought together representatives from the Thai FDA, the United States Department of Agriculture…
